user.title: General Settings
user.description: Step 1 of 4: Basic setup for this Smart Solar Manager profile.

data_description.name: Friendly profile name shown in Home Assistant.
data_description.scan_interval_minutes: How often the optimizer recalculates recommendations. Suggested: 10-30 minutes.

forecast.title: Forecast Inputs
forecast.description: Step 2 of 4: Connect Forecast.Solar entities. At least one field is required.

data_description.forecast_today_entity: Total expected solar production for today. Wh and kWh values are handled automatically.
data_description.forecast_remaining_today_entity: Remaining expected solar production for the rest of today. Wh and kWh values are handled automatically.
data_description.forecast_next_hour_entity: Expected solar production in the next hour. W, kW, and MW values are converted automatically.
data_description.forecast_tomorrow_entity: Total expected solar production for tomorrow. Wh and kWh values are handled automatically.

energy.title: Energy Signals
energy.description: Step 3 of 4: Add live power and battery entities used by the optimizer. Fields are pre-filled from your Energy Dashboard configuration where possible.

data_description.pv_power_entity: Current solar production power. W, kW, and MW values are converted automatically.
data_description.load_power_entity: Current house load/consumption power. W, kW, and MW values are converted automatically.
data_description.grid_import_entity: Grid import power. You can also use a signed net-grid sensor here: positive values are treated as import and negative values as export. W, kW, and MW values are converted automatically.
data_description.grid_export_entity: Grid export power. Optional if you only have an import sensor or a signed net-grid sensor. Positive values are treated as export and negative values as import. W, kW, and MW values are converted automatically.

control.title: Control and Overrides
control.description: Step 4 of 4: Select controllable devices for automation mode. A "Manual Override" switch will be created automatically for you.

data_description.controllable_devices: Select switch, climate, or other entities to control during solar surplus.

error.forecast_required: At least one forecast entity is required
error.invalid_interval: Interval must be between 5 and 120 minutes

abort.already_configured: Smart Solar Manager is already configured

data_description.auto_control_enabled: Allows execute_plan to apply actions automatically when conditions are met.
data_description.dry_run: When enabled, actions are simulated and no real service calls are executed.
data_description.mode_preset: Choose a preset to auto-fill optimisation weights, or select Custom to set them manually.
data_description.battery_min_soc: Minimum battery percentage to preserve as reserve.
data_description.grid_price: Estimated import price used for savings calculations.
data_description.action_max_retries: How many retries are attempted per action when a service call fails.
data_description.goal_cost_weight: Higher value prioritizes reducing electricity bill.
data_description.goal_self_consumption_weight: Higher value prioritizes using local solar energy directly.
data_description.goal_battery_health_weight: Higher value reduces aggressive battery cycling.
data_description.goal_grid_weight: Higher value emphasizes grid import/export balancing behavior.

customize.title: Custom Strategy Weights
customize.description: Fine-tune the optimisation weights. All values should be between 0 and 100.

data_description.goal_cost_weight: Higher value prioritizes reducing electricity bill.
data_description.goal_self_consumption_weight: Higher value prioritizes using local solar energy directly.
data_description.goal_battery_health_weight: Higher value reduces aggressive battery cycling.
data_description.goal_grid_weight: Higher value emphasizes grid import/export balancing behavior.
